title: --block-taking ops ignore the config-declared prefix->block mapping — neither input normalization nor error enrichment
description: Ops that take a --block name resolve it verbatim; a wrong-but-derivable name (--block task, --block decision) fails with a bare 'Block file not found' although config block_kinds declares both canonical_id and prefix, and expectedBlockForId (context-sdk.ts:1110) already computes TASK-046 -> tasks from it. The mapping is used neither to normalize the input (accept the singular / derive the block from a kind-prefixed id) nor to enrich the error (name the block that would have worked). resolve-item-by-id exists to bridge kind-id -> block, but the name-scoped readers' failure path never reaches for it. Class: every --block-taking op; triggering instance: read-block-item --block task. Full investigation: analysis/2026-06-10-block-name-vs-kind-id-resolution-gap.md.

evidence.reference: readBlockForDir raises the generic 'Block file not found' without consulting the prefix->block mapping
expectedBlockForId already derives the block from an id's kind-prefix via config block_kinds
agent investigation: root cause, verbatim repro, op enumeration, class verdict, prior-art (extends closed FGAP-032/FGAP-019 normalization precedents to the block-name axis)

impact: A natural-but-wrong block name costs a failed invocation and a help round-trip on every --block-taking op, although the substrate already holds the data to resolve or explain it.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
